description_long: This cowshed is an L-shaped, single-story agricultural building with a dark, hipped roof and wood-textured exterior walls. The structure features multiple rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass, evenly spaced along the longer wing, and two prominent black doors with simple cross-bracing. The roof is steeply pitched, providing efficient runoff, and the overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function over ornamentation. The wood paneling gives the building a rustic, rural appearance, while the windows and doors are symmetrically arranged for practicality. No visible ventilation features such as vents or cupolas are present, suggesting basic airflow management. The building is robust and likely constructed to withstand outdoor conditions, suitable for housing livestock in an urban or peri-urban context.
description: A large, L-shaped cowshed with a dark hipped roof, wood-paneled walls, multiple rectangular windows, and two black doors. The design is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for agricultural use.
description_view_0: Front and side of the L-shaped cowshed, showing two black doors and several windows on both wings.
description_view_1: Longer side of the cowshed with five evenly spaced windows and a single door at the far end.
description_view_2: Rear view of the longer wing, displaying five windows and uninterrupted wood paneling.
description_view_3: End of the shorter wing, showing two windows and the intersection of the hipped roof sections.
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows, evenly spaced on both wings
